role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for managing the referral to the Home Health Agency and coordinating the scheduling of the required home safety assessment with the vendor; the Patient Navigator's role is strictly to audit demographic accuracy in Atlantis, educate the family on the Healing Partners program to ensure caller ID recognition via the V-Card, and flag the pending assessment status to the Social Worker when it delays the transition.
narrative_summary: Mr. Harrison, a 79-year-old recovering from a right below-the-knee amputation, was clinically ready for discharge but faced a transition barrier due to a pending home safety assessment required for his wheelchair mobility. The discharge plan stalled as the Home Health Agency could not confirm a start-of-care date without the environmental check, leading to increased anxiety for his daughter. The Patient Navigator acted as a liaison by documenting the family's concerns, educating them on the transition process, and flagging the logistical delay in Atlantis to the Social Worker to ensure the first home visit remained a priority once the barrier was cleared.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the Atlantis referral queue against the facility face sheet. Discovered a discrepancy in the primary contact number for the patient's daughter; updated Atlantis with the correct mobile number to ensure the Medical Assistant can reach the family for the first home visit. Verified the discharge address is consistent with the location requested for the home safety evaluation.
v_card_flyer_status: Delivered the program flyer to the patient's bedside. Successfully verified that the V-Card was saved in the daughter's phone, explaining that this will identify the incoming Medical Assistant call as 'Healing Partners' to prevent missed communications during the critical 24-hour post-discharge window.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call with the daughter. The family's sentiment score was documented as 'Anxious' because they had not yet received a window for the safety assessment; this sentiment and the training gap regarding wheelchair transfers were flagged to the Social Worker for intervention.
atlantis_final_sync: Following the Social Worker's confirmation that the HHA had accepted the case after the safety assessment was completed, the discharge date and the first Medical Assistant visit were recorded in Atlantis. A physical appointment card was handed to Mr. Harrison prior to his transport home.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Mr. Harrison appears in the Atlantis queue following a right BKA; initial audit reveals a 'Pending' status for the Home Health Agency referral.
The Home Health Agency notifies the Social Worker that a home safety assessment is required before they can accept the referral, creating a logistical pause.
The patient's daughter expresses confusion to the Patient Navigator regarding why the discharge is delayed when the surgical site is healing well.
The Social Worker confirms via Atlantis that the safety assessment was successful and the HHA has moved to 'Accepted' status with a confirmed start-of-care date.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter is unaware of why the transition is delayed.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the standard requirement for a home safety assessment for amputation recovery.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis shows the HHA status as 'Pending' for three consecutive days.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the HHA acceptance stall to the Social Worker and documented the delay in the Atlantis timeline.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter expresses high anxiety about managing the wheelchair at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verified the family's sentiment score and documented the need for a joint meeting through the Social Worker.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Patient Navigator observes the 'Pending' status in the Atlantis portal and waits for the Social Worker to provide an update during the next morning's huddle.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the boundary by allowing the Social Worker to manage the vendor communication without interference, following the 'Wait' default.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Patient Navigator verifies the family's current readiness sentiment, educates them on the necessity of the safety assessment for their protection, and flags the persistent 'Pending' status to the Social Worker to see if the discharge date needs adjustment.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This uses liaison verbs to support the family and keep the Social Worker informed of the barrier without contacting the HHA directly.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Patient Navigator calls the Home Health Agency intake coordinator to ask for the results of the safety assessment and requests they expedite the start-of-care date so the Medical Assistant can be scheduled.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the No-Vendor Rule and the Verb Filter by attempting to coordinate logistics and investigate vendor delays directly.
